description: ML model predicting disease outbreak probability by Local Government Area using historical case and environmental data
primary_purpose: Public health outbreak prediction and early warning
data_inputs: Patient case reports; hospital admission data; climate data; population density data; LGA-level reporting
outputs: Risk probability score (Low/Medium/High/Critical) per LGA; alert notifications to NCDC epidemiologists
vendor_or_internal: External — Africa CDC / WHO AFRO technical support
affected_persons: General population — all 36 states and FCT
notes: System used in critical public health infrastructure. Outputs inform outbreak alerts and emergency resource deployment. Data quality gaps in rural LGA reporting identified. Model explainability limited — epidemiologists cannot interrogate prediction logic. Quarterly review recommended but not consistently implemented.

description: Computer vision model detecting anomalies in chest X-rays and CT scans; flags suspected TB, pneumonia, and cancer cases for radiologist review
business_unit: Radiology Department — Lagos University Teaching Hospital (LUTH)
data_inputs: Anonymised patient chest X-rays and CT scan images
outputs: Anomaly probability score per image; recommended review flag (Urgent/Routine/Clear)
notes: EU AI Act Annex III — AI used in medical device/diagnostic context. No formal conformity assessment conducted. Training data sourced primarily from non-Nigerian patient cohorts — potential bias in detection accuracy for Nigerian patient population not evaluated. No audit trail for AI recommendations. Mandatory radiologist review in place but not formally documented in governance policy.

description: Predictive model stratifying maternal mortality risk at antenatal registration and recommending specialist referral for high-risk pregnancies
business_unit: National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA)
primary_purpose: Maternal health risk stratification and referral
data_inputs: Age; parity; blood pressure; haemoglobin levels; gestation week; LGA location; facility type
outputs: Risk category (Low/Medium/High); referral recommendation to community health worker
affected_persons: Pregnant women at antenatal registration — 12 states active
geographic_scope: Nigeria — 12 states (North-West and South-West priority zones)
notes: EU AI Act Annex III — AI system used in healthcare affecting vulnerable population. Model trained predominantly on urban facility data; performance in rural Northern Nigerian settings not validated. Community Health Extension Workers (CHEWs) lack training to interpret or challenge AI outputs. No formal feedback mechanism to retrain model on updated Nigerian data. Review cycle of 24 months is inadequate given high-risk classification — bi-annual review recommended.

description: Health data aggregation and ML forecasting platform generating trend analysis and resource planning recommendations for health planners
business_unit: National Health Management Information System (NHMIS) — FMoH
primary_purpose: National health data analytics and resource planning support
data_inputs: Facility-level health service data; drug supply chain records; staffing data; disease burden statistics
outputs: Trend reports; demand forecasts; resource allocation recommendations (non-binding)
vendor_or_internal: External — University of Oslo (DHIS2); FMoH customisation team
affected_persons: Internal health planners and Ministry of Health officials
notes: Human decision-maker retains full control over all planning decisions — AI output is advisory only. Data completeness gaps from low-reporting facilities create risk of planning decisions based on incomplete forecasts. ML forecasting module added 2023 without formal governance review of upgraded functionality. Transparency of forecast methodology should be documented for health planners.

description: Conversational AI system providing autonomous symptom assessment and triage guidance to patients via SMS and WhatsApp; directs patients to appropriate facility level
primary_purpose: Patient-facing autonomous triage and health guidance
data_inputs: Patient-reported symptoms (text input); self-reported age; LGA location
outputs: Triage recommendation (Self-care/Primary Care/Emergency); facility referral suggestion
notes: CRITICAL GOVERNANCE GAP. Highest-risk system in this inventory. Autonomous AI system making healthcare triage recommendations directly to the general public with no mandatory human review at point of decision. Incorrect triage recommendation could directly delay emergency care. No accountable owner formally assigned within FMoH eHealth Division. No adverse event reporting mechanism in place. Vendor accountability for harmful outputs not addressed in current contract. Immediate governance review recommended — consider suspending autonomous operation pending appointment of accountable owner and introduction of human-in-the-loop protocols.

description: Machine learning model identifying likely contacts and transmission chains during disease outbreaks using call data records and case interview data
business_unit: Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC) — Surveillance Division
data_inputs: Anonymised call data records (CDR); case interview data; geographic location data
outputs: Ranked list of priority contacts for field follow-up; transmission chain visualisation
vendor_or_internal: External — Africa CDC emergency support tool
affected_persons: Individuals identified as potential disease contacts
notes: System activated during COVID-19 response and subsequent outbreak events. Use of call data records raises significant privacy and civil liberties concerns — data minimisation and consent protocols not publicly documented. System inactive between outbreaks but governance framework should be established prior to next activation. Data retention policy for CDR data not confirmed.
